[[File:Rod of ivandis detail.png|left|130px]]
The '''rod of Ivandis''' is a weapon created during the [[In Aid of the Myreque]] [[quest]]. As a silver weapon, it increases damage against [[vampyre (attribute)|vampyres]] by 10%. It is also one of the weapons that is capable of killing tier 2 vampyres. It also functions as a magic [[staff]] that can [[autocast]] spells from the [[Standard spellbook|Standard Spellbook]]. Its [[Special attacks|special attack]] can be used to fully defeat [[Vampyre Juveniles]] and [[Vampyre Juvinates]], at the cost of one of its 10 charges. After all charges are used, it turns into [[rod dust]]. 

The rod is created by casting [[Lvl-1 Enchant]] on a [[silvthrill rod]] and then dipping the enchanted rod into the well at [[Paterdomus]] using a [[rope]]. A silvthrill rod can be created by using a [[sapphire]], [[mithril bar]], and [[silver bar]] on a [[furnace]] with a [[rod mould]] in the [[inventory]]. 

The rod of Ivandis features in [[A Taste of Hope]], in which it is used along with a [[chain]] and an [[enchanted emerald sickle (b)]] to make the [[Ivandis flail]]. The Ivandis flail and [[Blisterwood flail|its upgraded version]] are the only weapons capable of damaging [[Vyrewatch]]. The weapon is a reconstruction of the one used by [[Ivandis Seergaze]], one of the [[Seven Priestly Warriors]].

==Special attack==
[[File:Retainer (rod).gif|frame|A player performs the rod of Ivandis' special attack, ''Retainer''.]]
The rod of Ivandis has a special attack, ''Retainer'', which traps a [[Vampyre Juvenile]] or [[Vampyre Juvinate]] that has less than 50% hitpoints remaining, preventing it from retaliating for 30 seconds. This consumes 10% of the player's special attack energy.

Each special attack consumes one charge from the rod. After all charges have been used, the player will be left with [[rod dust]] in their weapon slot.

Even if the attack fails to activate, stating either {{mes|This spell can only be used on vampyre juveniles or vampyre juvinates.}} or {{mes|This spell only works against vampyres with less than half of their health remaining.}}, it will still incur the normal attack delay, so take care not to accidentally use it in those circumstances.

==Treasure Trails==
{{FaloItem|rod of Ivandis|Vampyres are agile opponents, damaged best with a weapon of many components.}} Alternatively, an [[Ivandis flail]] or [[blisterwood flail]] can be used.

==Trivia==
*Once the rod of Ivandis has 2 charges remaining, the examine text changes to become {{mes|2! 2 charges left! Ha Ha Ha.}} This is a reference to {{WP|Count von Count}} of Sesame Street.
